source_title: Can You Paint Indoors During Monsoon? 5 Prep Tips
caption: Painting during monsoon in Manipur is not the disaster it sounds like, but you have to do five things first. Wall moisture is the silent killer of any June or July paint job; even a freshly plastered surface can hold enough humidity to blister a topcoat within weeks. At MAK-SHANG we moisture-test every wall before we open a tin. We also stagger coats by 8 to 10 hours during rainy weather, not the 4 hours written on the tin, because the tin was written for a climate that isn't ours. Waterproof primer is not optional here. Ukhrul's monsoon is long and persistent, and a premium emulsion without a sealed base is just expensive wallpaper. The five checks above are what we run through before every rainy-season job. Save this card before the next downpour.

The warm neutral wave reshaping European and Japanese interiors has a quieter, equally powerful version closer to home. Putty creams, almond whites, and soft ochre-grounded beiges are not beige in the flat, forgettable sense. They respond to morning light in Ukhrul the way warm stone does, and they sit naturally against the dark teakwood and woven cane that furnish most homes here. The trend translates here not because we copied a Pinterest board, but because the palette was already embedded in how this region builds and decorates. What changes when you apply it intentionally is the quality of stillness in a room. Share this with someone who thinks neutral means playing it safe.

source_title: Paint Finishes Explained: Warm Neutral Spectrum — Asian Paints Royale
caption: Warm neutrals are not all the same cream. Pale Ivory sits so close to white it catches light without declaring itself. Ivory adds a golden undertone that reads rich in afternoon sun. Beige Accent brings in enough warmth to anchor a room without the heaviness of deep colour. Beautiful Beige is where the palette starts to feel like a deliberate design choice rather than a default. These four Asian Paints Royale shades are the range we reach for when a client says nothing too bold, just something that feels considered. There's real difference between them in morning light versus evening light in an Ukhrul home. Save this side-by-side before your next colour consultation.
